text:
There once lived a humble squirrel named Benny. Benny loved collecting nuts every autumn. One year, he learned something new – storing nuts didn't just keep him warm. It fed hungry little birds.

As the days grew longer and colder, Benny gathered hundreds of nuts. He couldn't believe it. He stuffed them into every nook and cranny of his cozy burrow. Birds sung melodies through the crackles in the windowpane.

When winter hit with a blizzard's fury, Benny's nuts were his salvation. He gnawed on them until his teeth threatened to chatter. Yet life was good. Benny could eat enough to forget about woes.

Later that winter, as nature released its bounty during feasts for birds and beasts, Benny thought of his grateful forest mates. He gathered more nuts, planning snacks for everyone.

Spring returned, and Benny opened his treasures to find plenty to share. It wasn't just food; it was love.

His reputation for generosity grew. Soon, everyone knew
